title: Freshly Grilled Filipino Snapper Stir-fry
description: A fresh and energizing dish featuring grilled snapper fish with a creamy base made of fresh vegetables and aromatic spices.
instructions: Clean the snapper fillets thoroughly under running water.
Gently season the fish with salt, pepper, and a dash of soy sauce.
Finely chop the garlic cloves and finely slice the spring onions (scallions).
Peel and thinly slice the Thai chili peppers, leaving some seeds for added heat.
Wash and dice the baby carrots into bite-sized pieces. Core and dice the bell peppers.
In a wok or large skillet, heat up 2 tablespoons of sesame oil until it's hot but not smoking.
Add the sliced spring onions (scallions) to the pan first for color and aroma, followed by the diced garlic cloves.
Once the onions and garlic start to turn golden brown, add the Thai chili peppers. Stir-fry for about 1 minute.
Push aside the chopped vegetables from the wok and set them aside in a bowl.
Add more oil if necessary, then return the snapper fillets to the pan. Cook on each side until golden and slightly crispy, approximately 3-4 minutes per side.
Return the cooked vegetables back into the wok, along with any accumulated juices from the snapper.
Stir-fry for another minute until everything is combined well and the vegetables are heated through.
Serve hot as a main dish or side salad.
